segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Drake, wearing a dark button-up shirt and gold accessories, holding a circular 'I HAVE NEVER' sign on a white couch in a brightly lit studio. The scene quickly widens to show Drake, Jared Leto in a black shirt with rolled-up sleeves, and Ellen DeGeneres in a black blazer, seated around a rustic coffee table, engaging in a lively game. Ellen, acting as moderator, reads a "Never Have I Ever" question in her distinct voice, asking if anyone has "seen one of my Mordecai co-stars naked," which elicits gasps and cheers from the audience. Jared Leto responds with a clear "Yeah." Ellen then reads out team names like "Steelers, the Ravens, and the New York Giants" to more audience applause. A brief cut shows Sofia Vergara in a blue dress in a festive Christmas setting with a countdown timer, before returning to the studio where a graphic announces 'TOP 10 MOST-VIEWED CELEBRITIES PLAYING GAMES,' highlighting Drake and Jared Leto at number 10 with over 30 million views. The game continues with Ellen asking, "Never have I ever hooked up with a fan on tour," which sparks a massive, roaring reaction of cheers and laughter from the audience. Drake, laughing, attempts to clarify, "What what is a fan? Like someone that just enjoys your like...", while Jared interjects with "What?" and "Yeah, 'cause you don't..." amidst the continued uproar. Drake raises his 'I HAVE' sign, prompting Ellen to exclaim "Really?" and Jared to laughingly agree, "Yeah, yeah." Drake then humorously adds, "That's what they say. I know. Just having me out there." The jovial atmosphere persists as Ellen poses another question, "Never have I ever texted a nude selfie," leading to more audience gasps and laughter, with the 'JOIN OUR GAME NIGHT Club' logo visible throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment unfolds on a brightly lit talk show set, adorned with modern, nature-inspired wooden elements, potted greenery, and a cityscape backdrop featuring palm trees, as the on-screen graphic 'JOIN OUR GAME NIGHT Club' remains visible. Ellen DeGeneres, in a black blazer over a patterned blouse, moderates a lively game of 'I Have Never' with guests Drake and Jared Leto, who are seated on white armchairs around a wooden coffee table holding mugs and flowers. Ellen's cheerful voice guides the game, asking "Never have I ever made out with someone twice my own age," prompting laughter and applause from the audience. Drake, dressed in a dark button-up shirt and black pants, accessorized with a gold watch and bracelets, holds his circular 'I HAVE NEVER' sign, making a surprised "Oh" and later commenting in his relaxed voice, "Yeah, it's those kind of vibes, you know." The playful banter continues as Drake humorously asks, "You want me to give you guys a minute?" before Ellen poses the next question, "Never have I ever Googled myself." More laughter erupts, with Ellen incredulously asking, "Why would you do that? Just asking for trouble," to which Drake adds, "This is before we knew better." The trio shares continuous laughter as Ellen then asks, "Never have I ever sent a dirty text to the wrong person," eliciting a drawn-out "Ooh" from Jared and a humorous "The shame" from Drake, all contributing to the segment's dynamic and engaging atmosphere.

Laughter and applause fill the air, creating an upbeat atmosphere as they react animatedly. The scene then transitions to a festive holiday-themed '5 Second Rule' game. Ellen DeGeneres, still cheerful, now stands at a podium alongside Sofia Vergara, who is striking in a sleeveless blue dress with a cutout detail, against a backdrop of a decorated Christmas tree and wrapped gifts. A Game Host, wearing a green holiday sweater, asks Ellen to "name three famous Jennifers." Ellen, with her characteristic voice, quickly lists "Jennifer Lawrence, Jennifer Hudson," but falters on the third, prompting the host to declare "There's no Jennifer O'Neil." Sofia Vergara, with her distinct Colombian accent, interjects "Jennifer Lopez!" to more laughter. Ellen then humorously notes she "had 0.5 seconds to spare." The Game Host then challenges them to "name three things you do on a honeymoon." Sofia exclaims, "I knew, I knew you were behind this," followed by a buzzer sound as the timer runs out. Ellen laughs, saying "You can't concentrate!" Sofia then rapidly lists "Eat shrimp, eat shrimp, uh, shower, uh, dance, and uh, swim in the ocean," her accent adding to the humor. The host hits the buzzer, and Ellen, amused, asks, "Your first thing what you do is eat shrimp? Is that what you said?" Sofia, laughing, confirms, "I ate a lot of shrimp," concluding the playful and energetic game round with more laughter.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Sofia Vergara, in a sleeveless blue dress with a cutout detail, standing before a festive Christmas tree adorned with red and silver ornaments as a countdown timer ticks down from 3.0. The scene transitions to a wider shot showing Sofia, a Game Host in a green holiday sweater with candy cane and ornament details, and Ellen DeGeneres in a black blazer over a patterned blouse, all behind a white podium with a red buzzer for the '5 Second Rule' game. The Game Host prompts Sofia to "name three American sports teams," to which she quickly responds, "The Steelers, the Ravens, and the New York Giants," before a buzzer sounds, followed by laughter and applause. The Game Host declares, "All right, out of time, I win. And we'll be right back," with Ellen interjecting, "I am." The scene then dramatically shifts to a different game segment titled 'ONE EYED MONSTER!', featuring a vibrant, colorful stage with a large green monster prop with one eye and sharp teeth, set against a backdrop of jagged orange and blue patterns, illuminated by dynamic blue and green lighting and fog effects. Ellen DeGeneres, now at a silver podium with cue cards, moderates the game with 'Stranger Things Stars' Finn Wolfhard, Caleb McLaughlin, and Gaten Matarazzo, who stand behind individual podiums, smiling and attentive. Ellen explains the game, stating, "The game is quite, quite simple. I am going to ask you a question and uh, I'll even give you the answers. All of the answers are between zero and five." She illustrates with an example, asking, "how many eyes does a one-eyed monster have," to which Gaten Matarazzo quickly responds "One," which Ellen confirms. She humorously adds that if someone were "stupid" enough to guess "four," they would "have to walk over there and pull out three teeth because you were three away from the correct answer," eliciting laughter. Ellen further elaborates that "one of the teeth is a little more sensitive than the rest," and pulling it would cause the "one-eyed monster will swallow you whole," but "whoever survives will win the game." Ellen then asks the participants, "Ready?" before announcing, "All right, I am going to start with uh, Gaten and Noah."

A large, colorful monster prop with one blue eye and sharp yellow teeth dominates the center stage. The host directs Finn and Gaten, instructing "Go in there. Finn and Gaten retrieve large, beige, potato-shaped objects from the monster’s mouth, accompanied by sounds of struggle and a "Yuck!" from Finn, before returning to their podiums. The host then calls on Caleb, asking "Caleb, how many members are there in the Fifth Harmony?" to which Caleb enthusiastically responds "Ooh, five!" and "Five! It's five!" before realizing a rule clarification from another participant ("It's only one through four") leads him to exclaim "Oh, no!" The host continues to moderate with enthusiasm, instructing Caleb to "go pick one tooth," with Finn encouraging him, "You wanna do this one?" and another participant adding, "Just do it. Don't think, just do it." The segment maintains an energetic atmosphere with bright colors, playful props, and animated interactions.

segments.integrated_caption: The 'ONE EYED MONSTER!' game show segment begins with a distinct air release sound as Finn Wolfhard, in a black leather jacket, and Caleb McLaughlin, in a white shirt and blue vest, emerge from the large, colorful monster prop's open mouth, stepping onto a glowing circular platform. Gaten Matarazzo, wearing a brown jacket, stands at a podium with the game’s logo, while the cheerful Game Host, a woman in a black blazer, moderates from behind a silver podium. The lively green and blue backdrop features leafy panels and a large screen displaying the game’s title. The Host asks, "How many Emmys did Stranger Things win this year?" to which Gaten exclaims "Ha!", Finn answers "Five," and Caleb confirms "Five," which the Host validates as correct. The Host then introduces a fact about Caleb's past, stating he "was in Lion King on Broadway," before asking, "Up to how many miles away can a lion's roar be heard?" Caleb initially suggests "two miles," leading to a playful exchange where he challenges the Host about her knowledge of his two-year tenure in Lion King, while Gaten interjects, "That has nothing to do with anything." The Host firmly redirects, "Answer the question!" Finn defends, "We're thinking!" before Gaten offers "one," and Finn and Caleb agree, "We're gonna say one." The Host reveals the correct answer is "five," prompting laughter from everyone. Another air release sound accompanies the boys' position inside the monster's mouth, with Finn commenting, "That's a lot more than one tune," and Gaten adding, "Yeah, how about one two." The Host then moves the game forward, asking, "All right, who's going first? Caleb, you pick first."

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Ellen DeGeneres, seated in a white armchair on the right, in a black blazer over a patterned blouse, holding cue cards and moderating a lively game of 'Never Have I Ever'. With a cheerful demeanor, she asks, "Never have I ever fooled around in a bathroom during a party," prompting immediate audience laughter and applause. Seated beside her on a white couch are Justin Bieber, in a brown jacket, pink shirt, black pants, and a beige hat, holding a blue 'I HAVE NEVER' sign, and Madonna, dressed in a black outfit with red heart embellishments and sheer sleeves, holding a white 'I HAVE' sign. Ellen continues, noting the questions are "tame" before asking, "Never have I ever gotten kicked out of a bar." More laughter erupts, and Justin Bieber, with a youthful male voice, explains, "I just turned 21," repeating it as Ellen playfully urges him to "Be honest... Turn that thing around, Justin." The audience responds with cheers and applause. Ellen then prompts, "J...", to which Justin adds, "I swear." After a brief "Alright" from Ellen, she poses the next question: "Never have I ever dated someone and their sibling." This elicits another wave of audience laughter and applause as the guests react expressively, switching their signs. Madonna, with a distinct, slightly husky female voice, exclaims "Oh god" in amusement. Ellen then asks, "Never have I ever fooled around with someone else in the room," clarifying "with someone else in the room" and instructing them to "Wave your hands in the air." Madonna then comments, "I mean, all these questions are sexual," highlighting the playful and engaging atmosphere of the show, which is further emphasized by a persistent lower-third graphic reading 'JOIN OUR GAME NIGHT Club'.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a talk show set, featuring four women: Nicole Kidman, Margot Robbie, Charlize Theron, and Ellen DeGeneres. Ellen, seated in a white armchair, moderates a lighthearted game, gesturing animatedly. The backdrop displays a large pink speech bubble with 'Who Would Charlize Rather?' surrounded by red hearts. Ellen's energetic voice kicks off the game, telling Charlize not to 'feel sorry for her' as they'll find her 'somebody today.' Charlize, with a playful tone, interjects, 'I thought I was in a relationship with both of you,' eliciting laughter from the audience and guests. Nicole Kidman, in a calm voice, confirms, 'Yeah, yeah, she's not single,' to which Ellen clarifies that the show's staff are married and 'can't help you.' Ellen then explains they will show two men for Charlize to choose from, noting that the guests probably know Charlize 'a little better than I do.' The screen behind them changes to show 'DRAKE' and 'THE WEEKND'. Ellen suggests Drake is 'probably more her type,' prompting Charlize to playfully question, 'But am I the Drake's type?' and joke, 'I'm like, no, feel a little too old for him,' as she covers her face and laughs. Margot Robbie, with a clear Australian accent, chimes in, agreeing with a choice, while Nicole adds that The Weeknd (referred to by Ellen as Abel) is 'really nice.' Ellen agrees, and they decide to 'get rid of Drake.' Nicole then asks if the options are 'single,' to which Ellen humorously replies, 'Um, we don't know,' as the audience laughs. The screen then switches to 'MILO VENTIMIGLIA' and 'THE WEEKND', continuing the jovial atmosphere with Charlize smiling and gesturing. On-screen text 'JOIN OUR GAME NIGHT Club' and a view count of '33,606,200' from 'October 15, 2019' are visible.

Ellen, in a black blazer, moderates with animated gestures, while a large screen behind them displays celebrity headshots. Charlize, in a black blazer and white pants, playfully states, "I don't know him that well though," prompting Ellen to declare, "Okay, then let's get rid of Trevor," followed by collective "Oh!"s and laughter from the guests. Nicole, in a beige suit, then enthusiastically exclaims, "I adore him. He's amazing. I love him," likely referring to Michael B. Jordan, while Charlize adds, "No, I'm stay open." Ellen then suggests, "I think we're not gonna be able to top Michael," as the screen switches to Harry Styles and Michael B. Jordan, eliciting another round of "Oh!"s and laughter. Charlize dramatically declares, "I could be his mother! I literally could be his mother!" drawing more laughter, while Margot, in a brown leather dress, smiles. Nicole, leaning forward, suggests, "I bet you he likes older women," to which Charlize humorously retorts, "He's very mature. He's so incredibly mature." Nicole then seriously states, "I'm actually thinking that is a really good match," prompting Charlize to exclaim, "You're out of your mind, Nicole!" The game continues as Ellen presents the final choice, "Michael or Lenny Kravitz," with the screen displaying Lenny Kravitz and Michael B. Jordan. Nicole reacts positively, "Oh, Lenny Kravitz is so cool too," and confirms to Ellen, "I know Lenny a little bit," when asked if she knows him, maintaining the light-hearted and comedic atmosphere. Black 'ellen' mugs and a pink flower arrangement are visible on the coffee table, and a 'JOIN OUR GAME NIGHT Club' graphic overlay promotes viewer engagement.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a bright, dynamic studio set, featuring a large screen displaying 'BILL'S GROCERY BILLS' with a cartoon grocery store backdrop, as audience laughter and applause fill the air. Ellen DeGeneres, in a black blazer over a patterned blouse and holding cue cards, stands beside Bill Gates, who wears a red sweater. Ellen's clear, moderating voice welcomes viewers, explaining they will "test your knowledge of some everyday items that you get at the supermarket." A game participant, a man in a black hat and dark jacket, interacts with the stylized grocery checkout counter. When asked about his last supermarket visit, his calm male voice replies, "Long time ago." Ellen then outlines the game rules: guessing three out of five product prices within a dollar will win something for the audience, which elicits loud cheers. She adds, "If you get all of them, we'll pay off your children's student loans," prompting more laughter. Bill Gates, with a calm, amused tone, interjects, "No, no danger there," as Ellen introduces the first item. A whimsical chime sounds as a Rice-A-Roni box is placed in slot 1, which Ellen identifies as "the San Francisco treat." She asks the participant to guess its price, to which he confidently states, "$5," met with a slight audience murmur. After Bill observes the audience's reaction, a game show reveal sound plays, and Ellen exclaims, "Oh, it's a dollar!" as the '$1.00' price appears below the Rice-A-Roni box, drawing audience cheers. Throughout, the 'JOIN OUR GAME NIGHT Club' graphic remains visible, reinforcing the cheerful game-show atmosphere.

Ellen remarks, "Tide Pods. You've been hearing a lot about those lately," then asks for a price guess. The participant initially guesses "$4," then, encouraged to go higher, settles on "$10." A sound effect accompanies the reveal of the actual price: "$19.97," prompting Ellen to declare, "It's expensive to do laundry!" Bill Gates, raising his hand in mock surrender, quips, "I'll take a half," adding to the comedic atmosphere. Next, a box of Rice-A-Roni is placed in slot 1, its price of $1.00 visually revealed. The participant then places a container of dental floss in slot 3. Ellen emphasizes the importance of flossing, stating, "I floss as well. It's very important to keep your gums in good health." When asked to guess the floss price, the participant confidently states "$4." With a sound effect, the price of $3.78 is revealed, leading to cheers and applause from the audience, and Bill Gates exclaiming, "Nice!" segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with enthusiastic audience applause and cheering as Ellen DeGeneres, holding cue cards and wearing a black blazer, moderates 'BILL'S GROCERY BILLS' on a brightly lit, grocery store-themed set. She explains the game rules, stating, "You need to get both of these within a dollar so that they get something if not, you've got to run to your car," eliciting laughter from the audience. Beside her, Bill Gates, in a red sweater, smiles and observes. The Game Participant, in a black hat, stands at the stylized checkout counter with numbered slots, which displays a red Rice-A-Roni box in slot 1 and a red Tide pod in slot 2. Ellen then introduces a yellow bag of Totino’s Pizza Rolls in slot 4, saying, "Uh, Totino's Pizza Rolls. One bag of Totino's Pizza Rolls," accompanied by a game show chime. She playfully adds, "Totino's, have some tonight. Hot from the oven," as another chime sounds. Bill Gates then makes his price guess for the item, initially suggesting, "I'll go with uh, 22," which is met with murmurs from the audience. He quickly revises to, "No, no, 15, 15," and finally, "Eight." Ellen confirms, "Alright, let's try it. $8," before revealing the correct price, "$8.98!" A loud cheer erupts from the audience, accompanied by a success chime, as Ellen exclaims, "Yes! Two in a row!" The Game Participant gestures towards the items. Ellen then presents the next item, a box of Friday’s Spinach & Artichoke Cheese Dip in slot 5, asking, "How do you feel about this? This is TGI Fridays Frozen Spinach and Artichoke Dip," with another chime. She specifies, "It's 8 ounces. 8 ounces," to which Bill Gates thoughtfully comments, "It's branded," as the segment concludes with another chime. The 'JOIN OUR GAME NIGHT Club' graphic remains visible in the bottom left corner throughout, while the backdrop screen shows a cartoon grocery store scene.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens on a brightly lit studio set for the 'BILL'S GROCERY BILLS' game, featuring a large grocery checkout counter with numbered slots and product props like a Tide pod, Rice-A-Roni, Totino’s Pizza Rolls, and Friday’s Spinach & Artichoke Cheese Dip. Ellen DeGeneres, in a black blazer, moderates the game, holding cue cards, while Bill Gates, in a red sweater, stands beside her. A Game Participant, wearing a black hat, actively places product props into the numbered slots. The audience reacts with laughter and applause, visible against a backdrop screen displaying a cartoon grocery store scene with the game title. Ellen asks the audience if they think the grocery bill is "less than $10," to which they enthusiastically respond "Yes!" Bill Gates suggests "Like seven?" and later exclaims "Wow!" as Ellen and the audience call out various lower numbers, eventually settling on "four." A loud buzzer sounds, followed by intense audience cheers and applause, as Ellen exclaims, "Oh my God, thank you! All right! That was a big help." The segment then transitions to a different game, 'Who'd You Rather?', with Ellen now seated in a light pink suit on a white couch, joined by Trevor Noah, dressed in a black jacket. The screen behind them shows a pink heart-themed background with the game title, later displaying headshots of celebrities like Rihanna and Taylor Swift, then Rihanna and Tiffany Haddish. Trevor Noah reacts with expressive gestures and laughter, engaging in a lively discussion with Ellen. Ellen prompts Trevor to be "honest," and he readily agrees. When Rihanna's picture appears, Trevor humorously reveals they share the "exact same birthday," February 20th, and that he is a Pisces, joking about an "easy relationship." The set includes decorative white orchids, a pink flower arrangement, and Ellen mugs on the coffee table. Graphic overlays promote 'JOIN OUR GAME NIGHT Club' in the bottom left and display 'April 16, 2019' and '43,514,090 Views!' in the top right.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a lively 'Who'd You Rather?' game on The Ellen Show, featuring Ellen DeGeneres in a light pink suit and guest Trevor Noah in a black jacket, seated on white couches. A large, brightly lit screen behind them displays celebrity headshots, initially showing Rihanna and Tiffany Haddish. Trevor Noah, gesturing expressively, vocalizes his dilemma, stating, "This is hard," before deciding, "I would go, I'll switch to Tiffany Haddish now." Ellen, with playful gestures and a smile, asks "Really?" as Trevor explains his choice, humorously noting that with Rihanna, "after a while I get bored of the fact that everyone's like, 'It's Rihanna, it's Rihanna.' Who's that?" and emphasizing a desire for a "more equal" relationship, which elicits audience laughter. Ellen agrees, adding, "No, it has to be balanced." Trevor concludes his first choice, saying Tiffany Haddish "She's fun, she's funny, she's beautiful, I'll go with her." The screen then transitions to Khloe Kardashian and Tiffany Haddish, prompting Ellen to announce, "Right. Now we've got a Kardashian for you." Trevor reacts with a drawn-out "Oh," followed by more audience laughter. He then shares a "dirty secret," confessing, "I would have always loved to date a Kardashian just like for like a a week," which again sparks laughter from the audience and Ellen, who quips, "That's how long they last," and "That's actually the average." Trevor elaborates on his desire "just to be like in that world," wanting to "be on the show" and "in the game" as a Kardashian "just for a little bit." He then declares, "So I'll switch to Khloe. I'll go Khloe Kardashian," as Ellen responds, "Okay, good. I'm friendly with..." The coffee table between them holds black 'ellen' mugs and a pink flower arrangement, while a 'JOIN OUR GAME NIGHT Club' graphic overlay remains in the bottom left corner, all contributing to the energetic and humorous atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with audience laughter as One Direction members Liam Payne, Louis Tomlinson, Niall Horan, and Harry Styles are seated on a white couch, playing 'Never Have I Ever' with Ellen DeGeneres on her bright talk show set. Ellen, in a black blazer, asks about sending a wrong message, prompting Louis Tomlinson, in a black long-sleeve shirt, to respond with a British accent about the 'horrible moment' of sending an irreversible message. Harry Styles, in a leopard-print shirt, adds with a British accent, 'Just what the hell?' and Niall Horan, in a patterned short-sleeve shirt, chimes in with an Irish accent, 'Yeah.' Louis continues, explaining the need to preemptively address the recipient, leading Harry to humorously interject, 'Sorry, Dad,' which he then repeats, 'Sorry, Dad, that was for you,' to more laughter. Ellen then introduces the next prompt: 'Never have I ever lied about who I was.' Niall, with a smile, admits, 'I used to say I was Nick from Backstreet Boys,' drawing laughter. Louis, with a British accent, comments, 'You used to say...' while Harry playfully asks, 'Are you know, you know from One Direction?' Niall clarifies, 'No, I'm Nick from Backstreet Boys.' Ellen then reveals, 'You did say, you said you were Ellen for a while,' to which Louis responds, 'Yeah, yeah.' Ellen continues, 'I take that. And I say I'm you,' adding, 'And people come up to me,' as the audience laughs and applauds. The scene briefly cuts to a past 'Never Have I Ever' segment featuring Johnny Depp, Gwyneth Paltrow, and Paul Bettany, with a 'January 22, 2015' and '54,491,050 Views!' overlay, before returning to Ellen, who concludes, 'And I'll play along with you. I'm going to ask a question,' as she prepares for the next round.
